page: Microsoft Azure HX176-144rs is an x86_64 architecture server within the HX family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 144 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel processors and is equipped with 1408.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 9.78 GB of RAM per core.

This server configuration includes 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It is RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, which facilitates high-throughput, low-latency networking between nodes, though it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no onboard GPUs.

Given the high density of both memory and dedicated compute cores, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale memory-bound workloads and clustered environments where RDMA is utilized to minimize node-to-node latency. The hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive databases, large-scale scientific simulations, and high-performance computing workloads requiring substantial local storage and memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure HX176-144rs is an x86_64 architecture server in the HX family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 144 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel processors and 1408.0 GB of RAM, providing 9.78 GB of memory per core. The instance includes 4332 GB of local storage, supports Premium Storage, and features RDMA capabilities for low-latency networking. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This server is designed for memory-intensive and clustered workloads, such as large-scale databases, high-performance computing simulations, and memory-bound enterprise applications that benefit from high resource density and direct memory access networking.
